title: Add `systemd` build tag
body:
If the systemd development files are not present on the system which
builds podman, then `podman events` will error on runtime creation.

This commit mainly exists because projects which depend on libpod
would not need the podman event support and therefore do not need to
rely on the systemd headers.

Closes #3079

comments.body:
Hi @saschagrunert. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

> Maybe we can warn the user when the buildtags don't contain "systemd":
> `WARNING: Podman is being compiled without the systemd build tag. Install libsystemd for journald support`

Alright, I added a warning to the [`Makefile`](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3083/files#diff-b67911656ef5d18c4ae36cb6741b7965R32).

> @saschagrunert, is there a concrete user base for this build tag? I wonder which environments don't have or allow libsystemd.

I see two kind of users: One who rely on libpod as a development dependency. For example, I struggled a lot building systemd statically with musl libc for the CRI-O static musl build. The others are users which would like to use podman on Linux distributions which do not use systemd, like ArchBang, Alpine, Android or some Linux from scratch. There are [many more](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category:Linux_distributions_without_systemd) and I guess we should not forget some hardliners which replace systemd on their local distributions, too. :grin:

/approve
/ok-to-test

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I think we need a way of determining a safe default for `EventsLogger` in the libpod Runtime's config. A conditional-compiled function that returned "journald" if it was compiled in, "file" otherwise, would seem to work well?
changes LGTM, but the tests aren't hip

> I think we need a way of determining a safe default for `EventsLogger` in the libpod Runtime's config. A conditional-compiled function that returned "journald" if it was compiled in, "file" otherwise, would seem to work well?

I thought about something similar but considered it out of scope of this PR. Should I put it in here or follow up in an additional PR?
Does this PR Change the default if systemd is not compiled in?
what happens here if journald is the value for the event logger in the config file?
Then it should blow up.
I maintain podman on Void Linux and would appreciate this tag.
One more thing - can you comment out the `events_logger` line in `libpod.conf` by default, so we set sanely based on whether the tag is present?

> One more thing - can you comment out the `events_logger` line in `libpod.conf` by default, so we set sanely based on whether the tag is present?

Adapted it as suggested and rebased on top of the latest master branch.
@rhatdan yes, we (you) decided that journald should be the default.  I think you have captured the spirit in your summary.

> @saschagrunert If nothing is specified in libpod.conf, what does the tool choose? Does it fail?

Yes, it fails currently.

> @saschagrunert Does this behaviour work with this patch?

Yes, it should.

---

If the current behavior is not satisfying then I can see how to fallback to `file` if no journald is available, should I?
If someone specifies `journald` via config file and it's not available we should error - doing anything else is counter-intuitive. Current behavior works fine in my book.
@cevich Another timeout flake. It seems like it's consistently 30 minutes - but we're setting 90. This seems like it might be an infra thing?
@mheon ya, this looks like https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3114

Restarting failing tests.
These changes LGTM. I'd like to get them in for 1.3.1 this afternoon.
/lgtm
Fixes #3119
